items.title: Alice Springs' gruelling West Macs Monster ultra-marathon, in pictures

children.content: Over distances of up to 231km, 573 runners took to the Larapinta Trail outside Alice Springs at the weekend to compete in the 2026 West Macs Monster.

gallery.altText: A man runs along a ridge.
Runners move along a dirt track.

thumbnail.altText: A man runs along a ridge.
Runners move along a dirt track.

items.title: Some NT cases could halt if landmark legal challenge succeeds

children.content: Lawyers in the Northern Territory are commencing a landmark legal challenge that could require courts to halt some cases due to defendants being unable to access a lawyer amid a worsening legal aid crisis.

image.altText: A man with a serious expression sits on a long wooden table with legal books behind him.

items.title: Police make arrest over alleged stabbing of man sleeping at home in NT town

children.content: A 23-year-old man has been arrested over the alleged stabbing of another man who was sleeping in a home in remote Tennant Creek about 990 kilometres south-east of Darwin.

image.altText: A close-up shows flashing lights at night time on top of a police car.

items.title: Meet the women rangers in Central Australia working to protect country

children.content: Dozens of Central Land Council Aboriginal rangers gathered east of Alice Springs last week for the 2026 Women's Ranger Camp — a time for health, learning and healing in the wake of the town's recent loss of Kumanjayi Little Baby.

gallery.altText: A group of women trekking towards a rocky gorge, lush greenery surrounds them on either side.

thumbnail.altText: A group of women trekking towards a rocky gorge, lush greenery surrounds them on either side.

items.title: Amid grief, the NT's CLP rushes drastic child protection changes

children.content: Stakeholders have just one week to respond to sweeping changes to NT child protection laws that Aboriginal groups say could do "generations" of harm.

image.altText: A woman writes on a poster by candlelight

items.title: 'I can still feel him': Gurrumul's posthumous honour brings pride for family

children.content: Nearly a decade since his death, Gurrumul is set to be inducted into the ARIA Hall of Fame, bringing pride and reflection to a remote part of Australia.

image.altText: 'The Gurrumul documentary gives everyone in sight that no-one really had before,' says friend and manager Michael Hohnen

items.title: Advocates say 'three big gorillas' have stopped real national gambling reform

children.content: Under the cover of budget day, the Albanese government this week dropped its long-awaited response to a gambling harm inquiry. Advocates say it has "failed miserably".

image.altText: A Manly Sea Eagles jersey with rainbow bands above and below the sponsor name and on the cuffs and collar

items.title: Diphtheria is back in Australia. Here's what to know

children.content: Once a major cause of childhood deaths, diphtheria was largely eliminated through vaccination. Health authorities say falling immunity may now be helping the disease spread again.

image.altText: A young child gets there nose blown.

items.title: A Canberra woman's husband said not to book return flights. It was a case of exit trafficking

children.content: Advocates for migrant women say they're seeing more cases of exit trafficking, a little-known crime involving coercion, threats or deception to organise a person's departure from Australia.

gallery.altText: A clip art image of a woman, passport and cancelled stamp

thumbnail.altText: A clip art image of a woman, passport and cancelled stamp

items.title: Miles declares 'a win is a win' as Labor squeaks by in by-election

children.content: Labor candidate Luke Richmond will become the next member for Stafford, but the party's vote dropped several points.

image.altText: A man in a white buttoned shirt standing in front of press microphones with trees in the background.

avatar.alt: ABC Queensland state political reporter Jack McKay

items.title: Federal government intervenes for third time over China-linked stakes in rare earths miner

children.content: The federal treasurer orders another six companies to divest their holdings in Northern Minerals as concerns grow in Canberra that Chinese-backed investors are trying to seize control of the rare earths mining company.

image.altText: A sign explaining a new mine sits in red dirt as a person walks past in boots

items.title: Taiwan president responds after Trump's visit to China

children.content: Speaking after Donald Trump's meeting with China's president, Taiwan's leader says the island will not be sacrificed or traded and will not give up its free way of life.

image.altText: man smiles with green backdrop.

items.title: ASX down after Wall Street losses, AUD also lower, oil rises

children.content: The ASX 200 has opened the new trading week with a fall of 1.45 per cent, continuing its downward trend during the month of May. Meanwhile, uncertainty over the Iran war has seen Brent crude oil rise above $110 a barrel. Follow live.

image.altText: A woman is seen in the reflection of a window looking at shares' performance on a board of the ASX

items.title: For the second straight game, Slater has overlooked Walsh. But why?

children.content: Reece Walsh's generational run to end 2025 was almost without peer, but he has rejoined the ranks of the mortals to start this year.

articles.title: No Walsh for Queensland as Blues coach accidentally lists former Maroon

children.content: Billy Slater leaves Reece Walsh out of his squad for State of Origin I, while coach Laurie Daley accidentally announces the name of an ex-Queensland player.

image.altText: Casey McGuire and the Queensland Maroons at State of Origin training.

gallery.altText: Billy Slater talks to Reece Walsh on the sideline during a State of Origin game.

thumbnail.altText: Billy Slater talks to Reece Walsh on the sideline during a State of Origin game.

items.title: 'Boomer bashing': Are cuts to private health rebates addressing generational or wealth inequality?

children.content: The federal government plans to reduce private health insurance rebates for older Australians, a move one advocate fears could "tip many over the edge".

image.altText: An older woman in a grey jumper

items.title: Horror MotoGP crash leaves rider with broken neck

children.content: Alex Márquez is lucky to be alive after he spectacularly wrecked his MotoGP bike during the Catalan Grand Prix in Barcelona, while Johann Zarco was also taken to hospital after an equally terrifying crash later in the race.

image.altText: Alex Marquez crashes

items.title: Stronger mouse poison allowed for 'worst plague on record'

children.content: Farm groups have welcomed the chemical regulator's long-awaited decision to approve double-strength mouse baits amid plague numbers in Western Australia and South Australia.

image.altText: Three mice in farm shed.

items.title: Want to eat more beans? Try these easy and delicious recipes

children.content: From vegetarian chilli to hearty soup, these easy recipes might convince you to add beans to your regular dinner rotation.

image.altText: Bowl of vegetarian chilli topped with sour cream, avocado, coriander, jalapeno, a vegetarian dinner that freezes well.

items.title: Qld developer donation overhaul yields $170,000 in cash

children.content: More than $170,000 in donations from developers has been given to Queensland state politicians since laws banning developer donations were overturned in March.

gallery.altText: Jarrod Bleijie stands in front of an easel holding plans for a development.

thumbnail.altText: Jarrod Bleijie stands in front of an easel holding plans for a development.

items.title: How an Australian designer's fashion fame spurred a mental health unravelling

children.content: Rochelle Gregory has clothed everyone from Paris Hilton to Dua Lipa. Now the designer is using film to candidly explore her mental health battle while showcasing her latest collection.

image.altText: A woman in a black leather jacket struts down a runway while women clap.

items.title: Why the US, UK and NZ haven't raised interest rates in years

children.content: As Australians pay more on their mortgages, some countries have had no rise in their interest rate in recent years. So what's going on?

image.altText: A for sale sign sits outside a two-storey property with a white picket fence

items.title: Liberal senator speaks out against Taylor's budget reply

children.content: Senator Andrew McLachlan raises concerns over Angus Taylor's use of the term "mass immigration" and plan to push tax-paying migrants off welfare benefits.

image.altText: A man wearing a navy suit and tie with grey hair and a grey beard sitting in a grey room looks at the camera.

items.title: Nearly one in five Tasmanians would vote One Nation, poll shows

children.content: A recent Tasmanian poll has shown that One Nation would win a "number of seats" in the next state election.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson Farrer by-election

items.title: Inside the deadly search to recover four divers' bodies from a deep-sea Maldives cave

children.content: Expert divers have been sent to the Maldives to retrieve four bodies in a deep-sea cave network, in a search mission that's already killed one military rescue diver.

items.title: Man's leg amputated in public park, court hears

children.content: The trial of a man who allegedly performed an amputation on a 66-year-old, causing his death, is likely to focus on the accused's "state of mind" and intention, a court hears.

image.altText: A selfie image of a man wearing a black top on a tropical farm

items.title: Woman jailed for drunkenly setting friend on fire appeals sentence

children.content: Corbie Walpole's legal team has argued the sentencing judge failed to properly consider psychiatric evidence about her mental health at the time of the offending.

image.altText: Two women with sunglasses in the foreground and a man in the background

items.title: Plans for largest wind farm in Southern Hemisphere get Victorian approval

children.content: An environmental advocacy group and a council mayor say the Victorian government has not properly considered the potential impacts of building a major new wind farm in Western Victoria.

image.altText: wind farms in the background

items.title: 'This is the end': SA health chief recalls terrifying moment she was hit by car

children.content: SA public health chief Nicola Spurrier opens up about the moment when she was knocked from her bike by a car last year, and recounted the experience — which left her with broken bones and fearing for her life — only hours after a 39-year-old motorcyclist was killed in a crash in the Adelaide Hills.

image.altText: A woman pulls the top of her blouse aside, showing a 5-centimetre scar on her left shoulder

items.title: Mass showers and squalor: What spurred ex-Labor minister's NDIS fight

children.content: A former NSW Labor powerbroker and state disability minister pivotal in advocating for the establishment of the NDIS has warned against rushing through sweeping changes to the scheme.

items.title: What to know about the current Ebola outbreak in Uganda and the Congo

children.content: The WHO has declared a global health emergency after more than 80 cases of Ebola were confirmed across two African countries. Here's why the organisation is calling for preparedness, not panic.

children.content: The families of Palestinians believed to be trapped under the rubble of collapsed buildings want to bury their dead.

items.title: Pilots parachute from jets after mid-flight collision at US air show

items.title: French Epstein probe sees 10 new alleged victims come forward

children.content: About 10 new suspected victims have come forward as part of a French probe into the network of late US sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

children.content: Animal shelters across New South Wales sound the alarm on a worsening stray cat crisis.

items.title: Melbourne nightclub has operated for two years without insurance

children.content: Pride of our Footscray stopped paying public liability insurance when 18 insurers refused to underwrite the venue. The manager says operating without cover keeps staff on tenterhooks.
